fields.description:
## Why
An LLM reviewer can invent scale or inflate a small defect via a grand subject -- confident, blocking-eligible FALSE positives. These anchors constrain the reviewers' OWN reasoning (a calibration fix, not detection).

## What (concrete edit locations)
- G-9: small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning examples + two-directional bar in plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md.
- FP-3(b): make the "blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises" ratchet EXPLICIT + uniform. Edit locations confirmed: plan_review_verifier.md + plan_review_verifier_agentic.md prose, and the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius doc in review_kernel/verify.py. The ratchet phrase is currently absent from all three.
- Back-out: these are prompt/doc edits -- revert the commit (no expand-contract needed); note this in the ticket.

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A1/T5a carry the small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning + two-directional text. Proof: grep the phrases in both files.
- [ ] One-way blast_radius phrasing present in both verifier prompts + the SeverityAttrs doc. Proof: `grep -l "never raises" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier*.md src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py`.
- [ ] Eval covers BOTH directions: genuine high-scale/high-blast findings still fire AND inflated-scale findings get suppressed. Proof: two recall cases in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k scale; run it green.
